title: Define open module participation and workflow obligations
lane_outcome: Define a reviewed open module participation and workflow obligations model for #1392 so #1389 simplification does not hard-code today's first-party modules or fixed workflow slots.
purpose_for_parent: This is the prerequisite lane for #1389. It establishes how the recommended ordinary loop composes with repo workflow obligations and arbitrary module contributions before the visible-surface classification in #1390 and per-step simplification in #1391.
technical_strategy: Keep the first slice design-shaped. Inspect current contract, report, docs, and first-party module evidence; define the minimal participation vocabulary and optional extension points; map Planning, Memory, and Verification as examples; then route concrete contract/schema/doc changes into follow-on slices instead of changing internals prematurely.
technology_choices: Use checked-in issue-linked Planning as the durable owner for lane sequencing.
Use reviewed design evidence before product-source mutations.
Keep module participation generic; first-party modules are evidence, not the whole model.

slice_sequence.title: Define participation vocabulary and evidence map
slice_sequence.purpose_for_lane: Produce the reviewed design basis required by #1392: minimal participation contract, workflow-obligation composition, module contributions, conflict surfacing, startup exposure, and first-party module mapping.
slice_sequence.proof: Implemented contract/schema/runtime/docs/tests expose and validate participation_model; evidence cites module_registry, schema reference docs, docs/package/modules.md, docs/package/installed-surfaces.md, and Planning/Memory/Verification README surfaces.
slice_sequence.residual_after_slice: The #1392 model slice is complete. Deeper per-module participation fields, workflow-obligation provenance, and structured module reports remain optional follow-up only if #1390/#1391 or later #1389 slices need them.

slice_sequence.title: Route contract, schema, and docs follow-ups
slice_sequence.purpose_for_lane: Turn accepted design gaps into bounded implementation issues or execplans with exact touched surfaces and proof.
slice_sequence.proof: Each follow-up has an owner surface, changed-path proof expectation, and explicit dependency impact on #1390/#1391.
slice_sequence.residual_after_slice: The lane may close once #1390 and #1391 have enough criteria to proceed without re-litigating the participation model.

acceptance_boundary: This lane may be called complete only when #1392 has a reviewed open participation model that answers its acceptance questions, names follow-on contract/schema/doc criteria, and gives #1390/#1391 enough guidance to classify and simplify current surfaces without re-litigating architecture.
proof_strategy: Aggregate design-review evidence against #1392 acceptance first. Implementation proof is required only for follow-on contract/schema/doc slices that actually change product surfaces; lane proof must record any known gaps before closeout.

evidence: docs/package/modules.md and docs/package/installed-surfaces.md document the open participation boundary.
packages/planning/README.md, packages/memory/README.md, and packages/verification/README.md map first-party module examples.
tests/test_workspace_modules_cli.py asserts the modules CLI participation payload.
make test-workspace, make lint-workspace, make maintainer-surfaces, and make schema-reference-docs passed after closeout.
known_gaps: Per-module participation fields are not yet added to every module descriptor.
Workflow obligation reports do not yet carry stronger structured module/slot provenance.
Module reports remain weakly structured until a later simplification slice needs stricter fields.

residual_lane_work: Use this completed #1392 model as input to #1390 and #1391. Route deeper contract/schema/doc follow-ups only if those lanes cannot classify or simplify surfaces with the current participation vocabulary.
lane_to_epic_contribution: Defines the open operating-substrate boundary that the rest of #1389 must preserve while simplifying ordinary agent-facing workflow.

closeout_state.summary: #1392 model slice implemented; full #1389 simplification remains open.
closeout_state.residual_work: #1390 ordinary-loop classification, #1391 per-step simplification, future #1389 concrete reductions, and optional deeper provenance/report schema follow-ups if needed.

references.label: open module participation and workflow obligations model
ordinary continuity loop classification follows this lane

notes: Priority: first lane under #1389 because #1392 explicitly precedes #1390 and #1391. The model slice is implemented; do not start broad implementation cleanup from this lane alone.
